---
title: "Ross Wardrup"
subtitle: "Spatial & computational epidemiology · vector-borne disease · Bayesian spatiotemporal modeling"
description: "Ross Wardrup, researcher in spatial and computational epidemiology: infectious and vector-borne disease surveillance, environmental drivers, and Bayesian spatiotemporal modeling."
toc: false
---

::: {.lede}
I'm a researcher in spatial and computational epidemiology. I study how
infectious and vector-borne disease moves through populations and landscapes,
and I build the data pipelines and models to do it, from raw surveillance and
environmental data through to spatial and spatiotemporal inference.
:::

I came to epidemiology from medical geography and a decade of scientific
software work. I'm now finishing an MPH in Epidemiology at the University of
Memphis, where my thesis studies West Nile virus mosquito surveillance across
California. I also develop GIS and risk-modeling software for FEMA's Hazus
program, and I write open-source tools for spatial epidemiology in low-resource
settings. Throughout, I emphasize methodological rigor: spatial statistics done right,
analyses that account for how the data was actually collected, and results others
can reproduce.

## Selected work

::: {.project-grid}

::: {.project-card}
[West Nile virus thesis](projects/wnv-thesis.qmd){.card-title}

A quantitative study of WNV infection rate in California mosquito pools, and how
it tracks with environment, surveillance effort, and spatial pattern.

[Spatial epidemiology]{.tag} [Bayesian modeling]{.tag} [Surveillance data]{.tag}
:::

::: {.project-card}
[MESA](projects/mesa.qmd){.card-title}

A C++ desktop tool for public-health mapping in sparse, rural areas. Geocoding
and spatial cluster detection, checked against established libraries.

[Spatial statistics]{.tag} [C++]{.tag} [Public health]{.tag}
:::

::: {.project-card}
[FEMA Hazus](projects/hazus.qmd){.card-title}

GIS and risk-modeling software for FEMA's natural-hazard loss-estimation
program: hazard workflows, data engineering, and validation.

[GIS]{.tag} [Risk modeling]{.tag} [C#/C++]{.tag}
:::

::: {.project-card}
[SEUHD practicum](projects/seuhd-practicum.qmd){.card-title}

Applied public-health GIS at a local health department: respiratory-illness
hot-spot analysis and immunization-compliance reporting.

[Applied epidemiology]{.tag} [ArcGIS]{.tag} [Surveillance data]{.tag}
:::

:::

## Elsewhere

::: {.contact-strip}
**Ross Wardrup** &nbsp;·&nbsp;
[ross@rwardrup.com](mailto:ross@rwardrup.com) &nbsp;·&nbsp;
[github.com/minorsecond](https://github.com/minorsecond) &nbsp;·&nbsp;
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/robert-wardrup/) &nbsp;·&nbsp;
[ORCID](https://orcid.org/0009-0005-8235-6047) &nbsp;·&nbsp;
[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=NAcxI4IAAAAJ) &nbsp;·&nbsp;
[Curriculum vitae](cv.qmd)
:::
